reasoning: The primary anchor from the FRED quantitative model estimates a 0.62 probability of the CCC spread increasing over this short 7-9 day horizon, fueled by recent upward momentum and a historical seasonal pattern where spreads drift slightly higher in early April. However, short-horizon financial time series (like credit spreads) resemble random walks, and mean-reversion is a common dynamic following a significant spike (like the 29 bps jump to a 1-year high of 10.13 on March 27). Balancing the primary statistical anchor (0.62) against the baseline symmetric random walk expectation (~0.50) and risk of mean-reversion at a local maximum, I adjust slightly down to 0.61.
